title: A Wish
description: The night before the new school year begins, Adachi and Shimamura sit together, rubbing their hands in a playful ritual, hoping to be in the same class again.
content:
Adachi was crouched over, rubbing her hands together nonstop—shff shff shff—like she was chanting some strange spell.

What was she even doing this time? With that thought, I watched her from the side, more entertained than concerned.

From the kitchen came laughter—two of the voices light and quick. Probably eating snacks. I was quietly hoping they'd spare some for me. But for now, my attention stayed on Adachi, who had come over the day before the new school year started.

She kept rubbing her hands. Honestly, she'd been doing it all day, whenever she had even a second of downtime. She was really into whatever this was. If she kept that up, maybe she'd just… spontaneously combust?

After a yawn—probably triggered by the warm hint of spring—I finally decided to break the silence before drowsiness defeated me entirely.

Adachi, what are you doing?

I asked with a touch of anticipation, hoping for something amusing. In response, Adachi lifted her hands as if presenting the results of her strange ritual.

I'm praying… hoping we'll be in the same class again.

Through the small gap between her palms, I could see the tip of her cute little nose.

Class, huh… oh. Right. That was happening.

You mean the class shuffle. True, that's coming soon.

Every year when we move up a grade, the school reshuffles all the classes. So far, Adachi and I have been classmates two years straight. And tomorrow—we find out our final year's assignment.

How has Adachi handled this earth-shaking anticipation?

Answer: shff shff shff.

I really hope we end up together again.

Shimamura, you're saying it way too casually! Please take it seriously.

She protested softly. I realized this was a flaw of mine, and couldn't help reflecting a little.

But even if we're not, we can still see each other.

I mean, I'm sure she'd still come to find me. She'd probably show up at my classroom door every break, peeking in like a lost puppy.

Thinking about that made me feel a little guilty. Or more accurately—wouldn't that be a hassle for her? In that case, maybe it would be better if we got the same class. That realization finally let me understand her feelings properly.

But still… being together is better.

She said it like a child wanting affection, and I couldn't stop a small laugh from escaping.

Yeah. Having you there is definitely better than not.

Even if praying probably won't change the results.

Even so, Adachi tries anyway. She acts on her feelings and hopes, even when she doesn't know the outcome. There's something really precious about that.

Then I'll rub too. Like this—shff shff.

Shff… shff?

I sat down in front of her and copied her pose. Adachi resumed her hand-rubbing, now quietly mumbling shff shff with me.

The two of us looked like we were guarding or peeking at some invisible treasure, hands moving in sync.

What are we even doing…? The thought made a soft laugh bloom inside me. A gentle ripple spread in my chest, making this moment feel fuller, warmer—and making me feel a little fonder of the version of myself sitting here with her.

I think it'll work out, I said as our hands kept rubbing.

I really do feel like there's something like… fate between us.

Every moment I've spent with Adachi is soaked in her presence. When someone fills every corner of your world like that, it feels inevitable that we will cross paths again, no matter where we are.

As Yashiro would say, it's 'destiny~.'

But this 'destiny' is no ordinary fate.

Destiny didn't write the beginning for us. At the beginning, there was only Adachi and me. Adachi and Shimamura. It was Adachi who wove that first thread, who made the beginning happen.

If that's how things really are… I think I'd be happy beyond words.

Mm.

To her earnest, hopeful little wish, I nodded—softly, like spring sunlight warming the room.
